batches.name: infrastructure
batches.description: Parser struct, Parse(), advance/peek/match/expect/pos helpers, mapTokenType, ParseError

batches.name: names
batches.description: Identifiers, [bracketed identifiers], @variables, qualified names (server.db.schema.object)

batches.name: types
batches.description: Data types: INT, BIGINT, VARCHAR, NVARCHAR, DECIMAL, DATETIME2, UNIQUEIDENTIFIER, XML, etc.

batches.name: expressions
batches.description: All expression types: operators, CASE, BETWEEN, IN, LIKE, IS, IIF, COALESCE, NULLIF, CONVERT, CAST, TRY_CAST

batches.name: select
batches.description: SELECT with TOP, INTO, FOR XML/JSON, OPTION, CROSS/OUTER APPLY, CTE

batches.name: insert
batches.description: INSERT, OUTPUT clause, INSERT...EXEC

batches.name: update_delete
batches.description: UPDATE with FROM, DELETE with FROM, OUTPUT, TOP

batches.name: merge
batches.description: MERGE statement with WHEN MATCHED/NOT MATCHED

batches.name: create_table
batches.description: CREATE TABLE, constraints, IDENTITY, computed columns, temporal tables

batches.name: alter_table
batches.description: ALTER TABLE, ADD/DROP/ALTER COLUMN

batches.name: create_index
batches.description: CREATE INDEX, CLUSTERED/NONCLUSTERED, INCLUDE, WHERE, COLUMNSTORE

batches.name: create_view
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER VIEW, WITH SCHEMABINDING

batches.name: create_proc
batches.description: CREATE PROCEDURE/FUNCTION, parameters, RETURNS

batches.name: create_database
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP DATABASE

batches.name: drop
batches.description: DROP TABLE/INDEX/VIEW/PROC/FUNCTION/DATABASE

batches.name: control_flow
batches.description: IF/ELSE, WHILE, BEGIN/END, TRY/CATCH, GOTO, RETURN, BREAK, CONTINUE, WAITFOR

batches.name: declare_set
batches.description: DECLARE, SET, SELECT @var = expr

batches.name: transaction
batches.description: BEGIN TRAN, COMMIT, ROLLBACK, SAVE, @@TRANCOUNT

batches.name: execute
batches.description: EXEC/EXECUTE, sp_executesql

batches.name: grant
batches.description: GRANT, REVOKE, DENY, CREATE/ALTER/DROP USER/LOGIN/ROLE

batches.name: utility
batches.description: USE, PRINT, RAISERROR, THROW, DBCC, BULK INSERT, BACKUP/RESTORE

batches.name: go_batch
batches.description: GO batch separator handling

batches.name: stmt_dispatch
batches.description: Wire up all statements in parseStmt dispatcher

batches.name: cursor_operations
batches.description: DECLARE CURSOR (full syntax), OPEN, FETCH, CLOSE, DEALLOCATE cursor statements

batches.name: create_trigger
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER TRIGGER for DML triggers (AFTER/INSTEAD OF INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE) and DDL triggers

batches.name: create_schema
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER SCHEMA, AUTHORIZATION clause, TRANSFER

batches.name: create_type
batches.description: CREATE TYPE (alias types, table types, CLR types)

batches.name: create_sequence
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER SEQUENCE with START WITH, INCREMENT BY, MINVALUE, MAXVALUE, CYCLE, CACHE options

batches.name: create_synonym
batches.description: CREATE/DROP SYNONYM FOR target_object

batches.name: alter_objects
batches.description: ALTER DATABASE (SET options, MODIFY FILE, etc.), ALTER INDEX (REBUILD, REORGANIZE, DISABLE), ALTER VIEW, ALTER PROCEDURE, ALTER FUNCTION

batches.name: security_principals
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP USER, LOGIN, ROLE, APPLICATION ROLE. Batch 19 description claimed these but only GRANT/REVOKE/DENY were implemented

batches.name: security_keys_certs
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP CREDENTIAL, CERTIFICATE, SYMMETRIC KEY, ASYMMETRIC KEY, MASTER KEY

batches.name: dbcc
batches.description: DBCC commands: CHECKDB, CHECKTABLE, SHRINKDATABASE, SHRINKFILE, FREEPROCCACHE, DROPCLEANBUFFERS, SHOW_STATISTICS, INPUTBUFFER, OPENTRAN, etc.

batches.name: bulk_insert
batches.description: BULK INSERT table FROM file WITH options

batches.name: backup_restore
batches.description: BACKUP DATABASE/LOG, RESTORE DATABASE/LOG/HEADERONLY/FILELISTONLY with TO DISK/URL, WITH options

batches.name: pivot_unpivot
batches.description: PIVOT and UNPIVOT operators in FROM clause table sources

batches.name: tablesample
batches.description: TABLESAMPLE clause on table references in FROM

batches.name: rowset_functions
batches.description: OPENROWSET, OPENQUERY, OPENJSON, OPENDATASOURCE, OPENXML as table sources in FROM clause

batches.name: grouping_sets
batches.description: GROUPING SETS, CUBE, ROLLUP in GROUP BY clause, GROUPING() function

batches.name: begin_distributed_transaction
batches.description: BEGIN DISTRIBUTED TRANSACTION statement

batches.name: create_statistics
batches.description: CREATE/UPDATE/DROP STATISTICS on table columns with options (FULLSCAN, SAMPLE, NORECOMPUTE, INCREMENTAL)

batches.name: set_options
batches.description: SET session options: SET NOCOUNT ON/OFF, SET ANSI_NULLS ON/OFF, SET QUOTED_IDENTIFIER ON/OFF, SET XACT_ABORT ON/OFF, SET IDENTITY_INSERT table ON/OFF, SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL, SET LANGUAGE, SET DATEFORMAT, SET DATEFIRST, SET LOCK_TIMEOUT, SET ROWCOUNT, SET TEXTSIZE, SET ARITHABORT

batches.name: partition
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP PARTITION FUNCTION, CREATE/ALTER/DROP PARTITION SCHEME

batches.name: fulltext
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP FULLTEXT INDEX, CREATE/ALTER/DROP FULLTEXT CATALOG, CONTAINS/FREETEXT predicates in WHERE

batches.name: xml_schema_collection
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP XML SCHEMA COLLECTION

batches.name: assembly
batches.description: CREATE/ALTER/DROP ASSEMBLY for CLR integration

batches.name: service_broker
batches.description: Service Broker: CREATE/ALTER/DROP MESSAGE TYPE, CONTRACT, QUEUE, SERVICE, ROUTE, REMOTE SERVICE BINDING; SEND, RECEIVE, BEGIN/END CONVERSATION, GET CONVERSATION GROUP

batches.name: misc_utility
batches.description: CHECKPOINT, RECONFIGURE, SHUTDOWN, KILL, READTEXT, WRITETEXT, UPDATETEXT (filling gaps in batch 20 description)

batches.name: drop_extended
batches.description: Extend DROP to support: DROP SEQUENCE, DROP SYNONYM, DROP LOGIN, DROP USER, DROP ROLE, DROP ASSEMBLY, DROP PARTITION FUNCTION, DROP PARTITION SCHEME, DROP STATISTICS, DROP DEFAULT, DROP RULE, DROP XML SCHEMA COLLECTION, DROP FULLTEXT INDEX, DROP FULLTEXT CATALOG

batches.name: stmt_dispatch_phase2
batches.description: Wire up all new statement parsers (batches 23-48) into parseStmt and parseCreateStmt/parseAlterStmt dispatchers

batches.name: service_broker_missing
batches.description: Fix batch 46 gap: Implement missing CREATE ROUTE, CREATE REMOTE SERVICE BINDING, GET CONVERSATION GROUP parsers (rules listed but no functions exist)

batches.name: alter_trigger_enable_disable
batches.description: Fix batch 24 gap: Wire up ALTER TRIGGER in parseAlterStmt dispatcher + implement DISABLE TRIGGER and ENABLE TRIGGER statements

batches.name: service_broker_alter_drop
batches.description: New: ALTER/DROP for Service Broker objects (MESSAGE TYPE, CONTRACT, QUEUE, SERVICE, ROUTE, REMOTE SERVICE BINDING), CREATE/ALTER/DROP BROKER PRIORITY, MOVE CONVERSATION

batches.name: security_context
batches.description: New: EXECUTE AS (impersonation context switching), REVERT, ALTER AUTHORIZATION (ownership transfer)

batches.name: server_audit
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP SERVER AUDIT, SERVER AUDIT SPECIFICATION, DATABASE AUDIT SPECIFICATION

batches.name: endpoint
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP ENDPOINT with TCP/HTTP protocol options, SOAP/TSQL/SERVICE_BROKER/DATABASE_MIRRORING payloads

batches.name: event_notification_session
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP EVENT NOTIFICATION, CREATE/ALTER/DROP EVENT SESSION (Extended Events)

batches.name: external_objects
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP EXTERNAL DATA SOURCE, EXTERNAL TABLE, EXTERNAL FILE FORMAT for PolyBase

batches.name: encryption_keys
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP COLUMN ENCRYPTION KEY, COLUMN MASTER KEY, DATABASE ENCRYPTION KEY, DATABASE SCOPED CREDENTIAL, CRYPTOGRAPHIC PROVIDER

batches.name: resource_governor
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP WORKLOAD GROUP, RESOURCE POOL, EXTERNAL RESOURCE POOL, ALTER RESOURCE GOVERNOR

batches.name: server_level_objects
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP SERVER ROLE, ALTER SERVER CONFIGURATION, ALTER/BACKUP/RESTORE SERVICE MASTER KEY

batches.name: fulltext_stoplist_search
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP FULLTEXT STOPLIST, CREATE/ALTER/DROP SEARCH PROPERTY LIST

batches.name: security_policy_classification
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP SECURITY POLICY (row-level security), ADD/DROP SENSITIVITY CLASSIFICATION, ADD/DROP SIGNATURE

batches.name: specialized_indexes
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP XML INDEX, SELECTIVE XML INDEX, SPATIAL INDEX; CREATE/ALTER/DROP AGGREGATE (CLR)

batches.name: restore_extended_master_key
batches.description: New: RESTORE VERIFYONLY, RESTORE LABELONLY, RESTORE REWINDONLY; OPEN/CLOSE MASTER KEY; BACKUP/RESTORE MASTER KEY

batches.name: external_runtime
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP EXTERNAL LIBRARY, CREATE/ALTER/DROP EXTERNAL LANGUAGE

batches.name: availability_group
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP AVAILABILITY GROUP for Always On high availability

batches.name: deprecated_misc
batches.description: New: CREATE DEFAULT, CREATE RULE (deprecated but still valid T-SQL), ALTER DATABASE SCOPED CONFIGURATION

batches.name: stmt_dispatch_phase3
batches.description: Wire up all new statement parsers (batches 50-67) into parseStmt, parseCreateStmt, parseAlterStmt, and parseDropOrSecurityStmt dispatchers

batches.name: create_table_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 8 gap: add temporal table support (PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME, SYSTEM_VERSIONING), advanced column options (SPARSE, FILESTREAM, ROWGUIDCOL, ENCRYPTED WITH, MASKED WITH, HIDDEN, GENERATED ALWAYS AS ROW START/END), table-level storage (ON filegroup, TEXTIMAGE_ON, MEMORY_OPTIMIZED, DURABILITY)

batches.name: alter_table_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 9 gap: support comma-separated multiple ALTER TABLE actions, add missing action types (SWITCH PARTITION, CHECK/NOCHECK CONSTRAINT, ENABLE/DISABLE CHANGE_TRACKING, REBUILD PARTITION, SET FILESTREAM_ON/LOCK_ESCALATION), ALTER COLUMN depth (COLLATE, ADD/DROP MASKED, ADD/DROP ROWGUIDCOL, ADD/DROP PERSISTED)

batches.name: routine_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 12 gap: parse WITH options for CREATE/ALTER PROCEDURE (RECOMPILE, ENCRYPTION, EXECUTE AS) and CREATE/ALTER FUNCTION (SCHEMABINDING, RETURNS NULL ON NULL INPUT, CALLED ON NULL INPUT, EXECUTE AS, INLINE, NATIVE_COMPILATION). Fix batch 11 gap: parse CREATE VIEW WITH options (SCHEMABINDING, VIEW_METADATA, ENCRYPTION)

batches.name: service_broker_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 46 gap: structurally parse BEGIN CONVERSATION (FROM SERVICE, TO SERVICE, ON CONTRACT, WITH options), RECEIVE column list and INTO clause, contract message type definitions (SENT BY INITIATOR/TARGET/ANY), queue ACTIVATION options, END CONVERSATION WITH options (ERROR/DESCRIPTION)

batches.name: window_frame
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parse ROWS/RANGE/GROUPS window frame specification in OVER clause (BETWEEN UNBOUNDED PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW, N PRECEDING/FOLLOWING, etc.)

batches.name: for_xml_json_options
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: parse FOR XML options (TYPE, ROOT('name'), ELEMENTS XSINIL/ABSENT, XMLDATA, XMLSCHEMA), FOR JSON options (ROOT('name'), INCLUDE_NULL_VALUES, WITHOUT_ARRAY_WRAPPER)

batches.name: compound_assignment
batches.description: Fix batch 6/16 gap: support compound assignment operators (+=, -=, *=, /=, %=, &=, ^=, |=) in UPDATE SET clause and SET @variable statements

batches.name: create_database_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 13 gap: parse CREATE DATABASE full options (ON PRIMARY filegroup specs, LOG ON, COLLATE, WITH options, FOR ATTACH, AS SNAPSHOT OF, CONTAINMENT)

batches.name: alter_database_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 29 gap: replace ALTER DATABASE skip-loop with structured parsing of SET options, MODIFY FILE, ADD FILE/FILEGROUP, COLLATE, MODIFY NAME

batches.name: grant_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 19 gap: parse ON securable_class::name scope resolution syntax (e.g., GRANT SELECT ON SCHEMA::dbo), support REVOKE GRANT OPTION FOR

batches.name: subquery_comparison
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parse ANY/SOME/ALL subquery comparison operators (e.g., WHERE x > ALL (SELECT ...))

batches.name: workload_classifier
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP WORKLOAD CLASSIFIER (SQL Server 2019+) with WITH options (MEMBERNAME, WORKLOAD_GROUP, IMPORTANCE, etc.)

batches.name: create_type_index
batches.description: Fix batch 26 gap: parse INDEX clause in CREATE TYPE AS TABLE instead of skipping via skipToNextCommaOrParen(). BNF: INDEX index_name [CLUSTERED|NONCLUSTERED] (column [ASC|DESC] [,...n]) [INCLUDE (column,...n)]. Also add test coverage for CREATE TYPE (alias, CLR, table type with INDEX).

batches.name: alter_table_filetable
batches.description: Fix batch 70 gap: remove parseAlterTableActionLegacy stub, add ENABLE/DISABLE FILETABLE_NAMESPACE and SET FILETABLE_DIRECTORY support in ALTER TABLE ENABLE/DISABLE and SET handlers.

batches.name: table_hints
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: parse table hints in FROM clause (WITH (NOLOCK), WITH (ROWLOCK), WITH (UPDLOCK), WITH (HOLDLOCK), WITH (TABLOCK), WITH (PAGLOCK), WITH (INDEX(name)), WITH (FORCESEEK), WITH (SERIALIZABLE), WITH (READUNCOMMITTED), WITH (READCOMMITTED), WITH (REPEATABLEREAD), WITH (READPAST), WITH (XLOCK), WITH (SNAPSHOT)). Table hints appear after table references and optional aliases.

batches.name: collate_expression
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parse postfix COLLATE operator on expressions (expr COLLATE collation_name). This is a postfix operator that binds tightly, used in ORDER BY, WHERE, assignments, and column definitions.

batches.name: at_time_zone
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parse AT TIME ZONE postfix expression (datetime_expr AT TIME ZONE 'timezone_name'). Can be chained: expr AT TIME ZONE 'UTC' AT TIME ZONE 'Pacific Standard Time'.

batches.name: create_table_inline_index
batches.description: Fix batch 8 gap: parse inline INDEX definitions in CREATE TABLE body. BNF: INDEX index_name [UNIQUE] [CLUSTERED|NONCLUSTERED] (column [ASC|DESC] [,...n]) [INCLUDE (...)] [WHERE ...] [WITH (...)]. Currently only CONSTRAINT, PRIMARY, UNIQUE, CHECK, FOREIGN are parsed as table-level elements.

batches.name: graph_tables
batches.description: Fix batch 8 gap: parse CREATE TABLE ... AS NODE and CREATE TABLE ... AS EDGE for SQL Server graph database tables. Add IsNode/IsEdge flags to CreateTableStmt. Graph edge tables support $from_id, $to_id pseudo-columns.

batches.name: alter_table_add_period
batches.description: Fix batch 70 gap: parse ALTER TABLE ADD PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME (start_col, end_col) and ALTER TABLE DROP PERIOD FOR SYSTEM_TIME. These modify temporal table configuration.

batches.name: trigger_with_options
batches.description: Fix batch 24 gap: structurally parse CREATE/ALTER TRIGGER WITH options (ENCRYPTION, EXECUTE AS CALLER/SELF/OWNER/'user_name', NATIVE_COMPILATION, SCHEMABINDING) instead of skipping tokens between WITH and FOR/AFTER/INSTEAD.

batches.name: security_key_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 31 gap: replace skipSecurityKeyOptions() with structured parsing for CREATE/ALTER/DROP SYMMETRIC KEY (KEY_SOURCE, ALGORITHM, IDENTITY_VALUE, PROVIDER_KEY_NAME), ASYMMETRIC KEY (ALGORITHM, FILE, EXECUTABLE_FILE, ASSEMBLY, PROVIDER_KEY_NAME), CERTIFICATE (ENCRYPTION BY, WITH SUBJECT, EXPIRY_DATE, START_DATE), CREDENTIAL (IDENTITY, SECRET).

batches.name: choose_string_agg
batches.description: Fix batch 3 gap: parse CHOOSE(index, val1, val2, ...) function and STRING_AGG(expr, separator) WITHIN GROUP (ORDER BY ...) clause. WITHIN GROUP is a postfix clause on aggregate functions.

batches.name: option_query_hints
batches.description: Fix batch 4 gap: structurally parse OPTION clause query hints instead of generic expressions. Supported hints: RECOMPILE, OPTIMIZE FOR (@var = val | UNKNOWN), LOOP/HASH/MERGE JOIN, FORCE ORDER, MAXDOP n, MAXRECURSION n, USE PLAN N'xml', EXPAND VIEWS, FAST n, KEEP PLAN, KEEPFIXED PLAN, PARAMETERIZATION SIMPLE/FORCED, ROBUST PLAN.

batches.name: setuser_statement
batches.description: New: SETUSER statement (deprecated but valid T-SQL). Keyword kwSETUSER is defined in the lexer but not dispatched in parseStmt. BNF: SETUSER [ 'username' [ WITH NORESET ] ]

batches.name: begin_conversation_timer
batches.description: New: BEGIN CONVERSATION TIMER Service Broker statement. BNF: BEGIN CONVERSATION TIMER ( conversation_handle ) TIMEOUT = timeout_value

batches.name: alter_index_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 29 gap: replace skip-loop in parseAlterIndexStmt (line 574) with structured parsing of PARTITION = { n | ALL }, WITH ( option = value [, ...] ) for REBUILD/REORGANIZE actions. Options include FILLFACTOR, PAD_INDEX, SORT_IN_TEMPDB, STATISTICS_NORECOMPUTE, ONLINE, MAXDOP, DATA_COMPRESSION, RESUMABLE, MAX_DURATION.

batches.name: server_audit_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 54 gap: replace skip-loop in parseAuditOptions (line 278) with structured parsing. TO clause: { FILE ( FILEPATH = path [, MAXSIZE = n [MB|GB|TB] [, MAX_ROLLOVER_FILES = n] [, RESERVE_DISK_SPACE = ON|OFF] ) | APPLICATION_LOG | SECURITY_LOG | URL | EXTERNAL_MONITOR }. WITH clause: ( QUEUE_DELAY = n [, ON_FAILURE = CONTINUE|SHUTDOWN|FAIL_OPERATION] [, AUDIT_GUID = guid] [, STATE = ON|OFF] ). WHERE clause: event predicates.

batches.name: query_hint_table_hint_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 92 gap: parse TABLE HINT(exposed_object_name, hint [,...n]) in OPTION clause instead of skip-looping the parenthesized content (line 1484). exposed_object_name is a qualified table name; hints are standard table hints like NOLOCK, INDEX(), FORCESEEK.

batches.name: security_key_structured_options
batches.description: Fix batch 90 gap: replaced generic parseSecurityKeyOptions with type-specific parsers for ALTER SYMMETRIC KEY (ADD/DROP ENCRYPTION BY) and ALTER MASTER KEY ([FORCE] REGENERATE, ADD/DROP ENCRYPTION BY). Fixed OPEN SYMMETRIC KEY dispatch (identifier check). Added parseEncryptingMechanism helper for CERTIFICATE/PASSWORD/SYMMETRIC KEY/ASYMMETRIC KEY/SERVICE MASTER KEY mechanisms. Full BNF comments from official docs.

batches.name: endpoint_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 55 gap: replace skip-loop in parseEndpointOptions (line 101) with structured parsing of CREATE/ALTER ENDPOINT options. STATE = STARTED|STOPPED|DISABLED, AUTHORIZATION owner, AS { TCP | HTTP } ( protocol_options ), FOR { TSQL | SERVICE_BROKER | DATABASE_MIRRORING | SOAP } ( payload_options ). TCP options: LISTENER_PORT, LISTENER_IP. HTTP options: PATH, AUTHENTICATION, PORTS, SITE, CLEAR_PORT, SSL_PORT, AUTH_REALM, DEFAULT_LOGON_DOMAIN, COMPRESSION.

batches.name: service_broker_alter_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 52 gap: replace skip-loop in parseServiceBrokerOptions WITH clause (line 1024) with structured parsing. ALTER MESSAGE TYPE: VALIDATION = { NONE | EMPTY | WELL_FORMED_XML | VALID_XML WITH SCHEMA COLLECTION name }. ALTER CONTRACT: ADD/DROP MESSAGE TYPE name SENT BY { INITIATOR | TARGET | ANY }. ALTER ROUTE: WITH SERVICE_NAME, BROKER_INSTANCE, LIFETIME, ADDRESS, MIRROR_ADDRESS options. ALTER REMOTE SERVICE BINDING: WITH USER, ANONYMOUS = { ON | OFF } options.

batches.name: event_session_specs_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 56 gap: replace skip-loops in parseEventSessionEventSpec (line 392) and parseEventSessionTargetSpec (line 421) with structured parsing. Event spec SET: ( SET column = value [,...] ), ACTION: ( package.action [,...] ), WHERE: ( predicate ). Target spec SET: ( SET column = value [,...] ). Values can be quoted strings, integers, or identifiers.

batches.name: resource_governor_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 59 gap: replace semi-generic skip-loop in parseResourceGovernorOptions (line 325) with structured parsing. Used by 9 callers: CREATE/ALTER WORKLOAD GROUP (REQUEST_MAX_MEMORY_GRANT_PERCENT, REQUEST_MAX_CPU_TIME_SEC, MAX_DOP, GROUP_MAX_REQUESTS, IMPORTANCE), CREATE/ALTER RESOURCE POOL (MIN_CPU_PERCENT, MAX_CPU_PERCENT, CAP_CPU_PERCENT, MIN_MEMORY_PERCENT, MAX_MEMORY_PERCENT, MIN_IOPS_PER_VOLUME, MAX_IOPS_PER_VOLUME, AFFINITY), CREATE/ALTER EXTERNAL RESOURCE POOL, ALTER RESOURCE GOVERNOR (RECONFIGURE, DISABLE, RESET STATISTICS, CLASSIFIER_FUNCTION), CREATE/ALTER/DROP WORKLOAD CLASSIFIER (MEMBERNAME, WORKLOAD_GROUP, IMPORTANCE, START_TIME, END_TIME, LABEL, CONTEXT).

batches.name: drop_security_keys_dispatch
batches.description: Fix batch 31 gap: add DROP MASTER KEY, DROP CREDENTIAL, DROP CERTIFICATE, DROP ASYMMETRIC KEY, DROP SYMMETRIC KEY to parseDropOrSecurityStmt dispatcher and implement parsers. These objects have CREATE/ALTER support but missing DROP dispatch.

batches.name: drop_external_library_language_dispatch
batches.description: Fix batch 65 gap: wire DROP EXTERNAL LIBRARY and DROP EXTERNAL LANGUAGE to their existing parseDropExternalLibraryStmt/parseDropExternalLanguageStmt parsers in parseDropOrSecurityStmt dispatcher. Parsers exist but are unreachable from the dispatcher.

batches.name: backup_restore_symmetric_key
batches.description: New: BACKUP SYMMETRIC KEY and RESTORE SYMMETRIC KEY statements. BNF: BACKUP SYMMETRIC KEY key_name TO FILE = 'path' ENCRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'password'; RESTORE SYMMETRIC KEY key_name FROM FILE = 'path' DECRYPTION BY PASSWORD = 'password'.

batches.name: get_transmission_status
batches.description: New: GET TRANSMISSION STATUS Service Broker statement. BNF: GET TRANSMISSION STATUS ( conversation_handle )

batches.name: endpoint_unknown_protocol_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 99 gap: replace skipParenthesized() call at line 179 for non-TCP protocols with structured parsing. HTTP protocol options (PATH, AUTHENTICATION, PORTS, SITE, CLEAR_PORT, SSL_PORT, AUTH_REALM, DEFAULT_LOGON_DOMAIN, COMPRESSION) should be parsed structurally.

batches.name: external_model
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP EXTERNAL MODEL for machine learning model management (SQL Server 2022+). BNF: CREATE EXTERNAL MODEL model_name WITH ( LOCATION = 'path', DATA_SOURCE = source, FORMAT = 'ONNX' )

batches.name: json_vector_index
batches.description: New: CREATE JSON INDEX and CREATE VECTOR INDEX (SQL Server 2025+). JSON INDEX indexes JSON documents; VECTOR INDEX supports approximate nearest neighbor search with options (TYPE = DISKANN, DISTANCE = COSINE/DOT/L2)

batches.name: materialized_view
batches.description: New: CREATE MATERIALIZED VIEW AS SELECT, ALTER MATERIALIZED VIEW (REBUILD/DISABLE), DROP MATERIALIZED VIEW (Azure Synapse/Fabric). WITH (DISTRIBUTION = HASH/ROUND_ROBIN) option

batches.name: copy_into
batches.description: New: COPY INTO statement for bulk data loading (Azure Synapse/Fabric). BNF: COPY INTO table_name [ (column_list) ] FROM 'external_location' [,...n] WITH ( option = value [,...n] )

batches.name: rename_cetas
batches.description: New: RENAME statement (Azure Synapse/Fabric), CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE AS SELECT (CETAS), CREATE TABLE AS CLONE OF (Azure). RENAME OBJECT :: schema.old_name TO new_name

batches.name: stmt_dispatch_phase4
batches.description: Wire up all new statement parsers (batches 108-112) into parseStmt, parseCreateStmt, parseAlterStmt, and parseDropOrSecurityStmt dispatchers

batches.name: server_config_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 60 gap: replace consumeServerConfigOptions() generic token-to-string loop (line 255) with structured parsing. ALTER SERVER CONFIGURATION SET options: PROCESS AFFINITY (CPU/NUMANODE ranges), DIAGNOSTICS LOG (ON/OFF/PATH/MAX_SIZE/MAX_FILES), HADR CLUSTER CONTEXT, BUFFER POOL EXTENSION (ON/OFF/FILENAME/SIZE), SOFTNUMA (ON/OFF), EXTERNAL_SCRIPT (ON/OFF), FAILOVER CLUSTER PROPERTY. Each option type has distinct sub-syntax.

batches.name: availability_group_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 66 gap: replace generic string collection in parseAvailabilityGroupOptions() (line 218) with structured parsing. REPLICA ON: 'server' WITH (ENDPOINT_URL, AVAILABILITY_MODE, FAILOVER_MODE, SEEDING_MODE, SECONDARY_ROLE, PRIMARY_ROLE, SESSION_TIMEOUT, BACKUP_PRIORITY). LISTENER: 'name' WITH (DHCP/IP addresses, PORT). parseNestedParens() calls at lines 225, 400 should be replaced with typed option nodes.

batches.name: endpoint_unknown_protocol_skip
batches.description: Fix batch 107 gap: remaining skipParenthesized() call at line 183 in parseEndpointOptions() for non-TCP/non-HTTP protocols. Should parse the parenthesized options structurally as key=value pairs or raise a parse error for truly unknown protocols.

batches.name: alter_database_unknown_action
batches.description: Fix batch 77 gap: replace token skip-loop at line 134 in parseAlterDatabaseStmt() default case with structured parsing. Missing ALTER DATABASE actions: REMOVE FILE/FILEGROUP, SET PARTNER (mirroring), SET WITNESS, SET HADR. Also fix ALTER INDEX unknown token skip at line 707 in parseAlterIndexOptions().

batches.name: predict_statement
batches.description: New: PREDICT statement (SQL Server 2022+). BNF: PREDICT ( MODEL = @model | 'literal' , DATA = table_source [ , RUNTIME = ONNX ] ) WITH ( column_def [,...n] ). Used for native ML model scoring.

batches.name: edge_constraint
batches.description: New: CREATE/ALTER/DROP EDGE CONSTRAINT for SQL Server graph tables. BNF: CONSTRAINT constraint_name EDGE CONSTRAINT ( { node_table TO node_table | CONNECTION ( node_table TO node_table [,...n] ) } ). ALTER TABLE ADD/DROP CONSTRAINT for edge constraints. Graph tables (AS EDGE) support EDGE CONSTRAINT but parser only has AS NODE/AS EDGE flags.

batches.name: backup_restore_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 34 gap: replace generic string-accumulation loop in parseBackupRestoreOptions() with structured parsing. BACKUP options: COMPRESSION, NO_COMPRESSION, ENCRYPTION (ALGORITHM, SERVER CERTIFICATE/ASYMMETRIC KEY), DIFFERENTIAL, COPY_ONLY, DESCRIPTION, NAME, EXPIREDATE, RETAINDAYS, INIT/NOINIT, NOSKIP/SKIP, FORMAT/NOFORMAT, MEDIADESCRIPTION, MEDIANAME, BLOCKSIZE, BUFFERCOUNT, MAXTRANSFERSIZE, NO_CHECKSUM/CHECKSUM, STOP_ON_ERROR/CONTINUE_AFTER_ERROR, STATS, RESTART. RESTORE options: MOVE 'logical' TO 'physical', REPLACE, RECOVERY/NORECOVERY/STANDBY, STOPAT, STOPATMARK, STOPBEFOREMARK, FILE, MEDIANAME, MEDIAPASSWORD, ENABLE_BROKER, NEW_BROKER, ERROR_BROKER_CONVERSATIONS.

batches.name: create_database_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 76 gap: replace generic string-building loops in parseDatabaseWithOptions() and parseDatabaseAttachOptions() with structured parsing. WITH options: DB_CHAINING ON/OFF, TRUSTWORTHY ON/OFF, DEFAULT_FULLTEXT_LANGUAGE, DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, NESTED_TRIGGERS, TRANSFORM_NOISE_WORDS, TWO_DIGIT_YEAR_CUTOFF, CATALOG_COLLATION, LEDGER = ON/OFF, PERSISTENT_LOG_BUFFER = ON. FILESTREAM sub-options: NON_TRANSACTED_ACCESS = OFF/READ_ONLY/FULL, DIRECTORY_NAME = 'name'. Attach options: ENABLE_BROKER, NEW_BROKER, ERROR_BROKER_CONVERSATIONS, RESTRICTED_USER.

batches.name: security_principal_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 30 gap: replace generic key=value loop in parseSecurityPrincipalWithOptions() with type-specific structured parsing. CREATE USER: WITH PASSWORD, DEFAULT_SCHEMA, DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, ALLOW_ENCRYPTED_VALUE_MODIFICATIONS, SID. CREATE LOGIN: WITH PASSWORD (MUST_CHANGE, HASHED), DEFAULT_DATABASE, DEFAULT_LANGUAGE, CHECK_EXPIRATION, CHECK_POLICY, CREDENTIAL, SID, FROM WINDOWS/CERTIFICATE/ASYMMETRIC KEY/EXTERNAL PROVIDER. CREATE ROLE: AUTHORIZATION. CREATE APPLICATION ROLE: WITH PASSWORD, DEFAULT_SCHEMA.

batches.name: dbcc_with_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 32 gap: replace generic identifier loop in DBCC WITH options parsing with structured parsing. Common DBCC WITH options: NO_INFOMSGS, ALL_ERRORMSGS, PHYSICAL_ONLY, EXTENDED_LOGICAL_CHECKS, DATA_PURITY, TABLOCK, ESTIMATEONLY, COUNT_ROWS. The DBCC arguments themselves (inside parentheses) are already parsed as expressions, but the WITH clause options are just collected as string identifiers.

batches.name: external_nested_parens_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 57/65 gap: replace 7 parseNestedParens() calls in external library/language/option parsing with structured parsing. Lines 400, 457, 514, 520, 578 (library/language FROM/SET/ADD clause file specs with CONTENT/FILENAME/PATH sub-options), line 715 (FORMAT_OPTIONS parenthesized list), line 761 (SHARDED function syntax). Also replace string builder in CREATE EXTERNAL TABLE column parsing at lines 222-245 with structured column definition nodes.

batches.name: availability_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 115 gap: replace 2 parseNestedParens() calls at lines 483, 503 in parseParenthesizedAGOptions() with structured IP address tuple and key=value list parsing. Replace manual string concatenation for LISTENER/ADD LISTENER/REMOVE LISTENER/MODIFY LISTENER/RESTART LISTENER options at lines 268-346, 408-413 with structured AST nodes. Fix GRANT/DENY CREATE ANY DATABASE token skip at lines 380-390.

batches.name: server_config_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 114 gap: replace consumeServerConfigOptions() generic token-to-String fallback at lines 689-727 (called from line 254) with structured option parsing per config type. Fix buffer pool extension non-identifier token skip at lines 483-519. Fix SUSPEND_FOR_SNAPSHOT_BACKUP database list string accumulation at lines 648-654 and unknown token skip at line 667.

batches.name: select_query_hint_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 92/97 gap: replace strings.Builder in parseQueryHint() at lines 1268-1591 with structured QueryHint AST nodes (typed hint kind + typed parameters). Replace manual parenthesis depth tracking at lines 1522-1545 for TABLE HINT args with parseTableHint() reuse. Fix fallback expression parsing at lines 1565-1585 for unknown hints with proper error or structured unknown hint node.

batches.name: security_keys_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 98 gap: replace strings.Builder in parseSecurityKeyStmtColumn() at lines 353-376 with structured ADD/DROP VALUE AST nodes. Replace parseSecurityKeyOptions() string accumulation at lines 675-702 and unstructured for-loop at lines 645-753 with structured ENCRYPTION BY / DECRYPTION BY mechanism nodes reusing parseEncryptingMechanism().

batches.name: fulltext_alter_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 43 gap: replace strings.Builder action accumulation in parseAlterFulltextIndexStmt() at lines 165-178 with structured action type enum (ADD, DROP, SET, START/STOP, ENABLE/DISABLE, etc.). Fix batch 61 gap: replace unknown option skip in parseAlterSearchPropertyListStmt() WITH clause at lines 567-572 with structured property option parsing.

batches.name: security_misc_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 62 gap: replace strings.Builder in parseSignatureCryptoItem() at lines 511-560 with structured CryptoItem AST node (mechanism type, name, optional password/signature). Fix token loop in parseAddSensitivityClassificationStmt() at lines 349-369 with structured label/information_type/rank option parsing.

batches.name: utility_cetas_dbscoped_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 112 gap: replace strings.Builder with manual depth tracking in parseCreateExternalTableAsSelectStmt() column parsing at lines 1077-1104 with structured column definition list. Fix batch 67 gap: replace string accumulation in parseAlterDatabaseScopedConfigStmt() at lines 622-679 with structured SET option = value nodes.

batches.name: alter_database_unknown_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 117 gap: replace parseNestedParens() call at line 140 and mixed skip-loop at lines 137-146 in parseAlterDatabaseStmt() unknown action handler with structured parsing or explicit error for unsupported actions. Replace string concatenation in parseAlterDatabaseSubOptions() at lines 448-476 and parseAlterDatabaseTermination() at lines 498-508 with structured AST nodes.

batches.name: endpoint_remaining_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 116 gap: replace parseNestedParens() call at line 299 in parseEndpointGenericProtocolOptions() with structured key=value pair parsing. Remove skipParenthesized() function (lines 602-620) and replace its usage. Fix generic p.advance() skips for unknown tokens at lines 212-213 in parseEndpointOptions() and line 490 in parseEndpointPayloadOptions().

batches.name: declare_set_isolation_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 41 gap: replace strings.Builder in SET TRANSACTION ISOLATION LEVEL parsing at lines 173-180 with structured isolation level enum node (READ UNCOMMITTED, READ COMMITTED, REPEATABLE READ, SERIALIZABLE, SNAPSHOT) instead of raw string accumulation.

batches.name: misc_unknown_skip_depth
batches.description: Fix remaining shallow unknown-token-skip patterns across multiple files: create_table.go:280-281 (SYSTEM_VERSIONING sub-option value consumed without validation), alter_table.go:447-449 (ALTER COLUMN unknown option consumed as string), event.go:145 (event notification unknown token p.advance()), backup_restore.go:381 (unknown backup/restore option treated as flag).

batches.name: alter_authorization_entity_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 53 gap: replace token accumulation loop in parseAlterAuthorizationStmt() at lines 593-609 with structured entity type parsing. BNF: ALTER AUTHORIZATION ON [ entity_type :: ] entity_name TO { principal_name | SCHEMA OWNER }. Entity types: OBJECT, SCHEMA, DATABASE, ASSEMBLY, ROLE, TYPE, XML SCHEMA COLLECTION, etc.

batches.name: ctas_synapse
batches.description: New: CREATE TABLE AS SELECT (CTAS) for Azure Synapse Analytics. BNF: CREATE TABLE table_name [ WITH ( DISTRIBUTION = { HASH(col) | ROUND_ROBIN | REPLICATE }, [ CLUSTERED COLUMNSTORE INDEX | CLUSTERED INDEX (col,...) | HEAP ] [, PARTITION (col RANGE ...)] ) ] AS select_stmt

batches.name: audit_spec_actions_where_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 96 gap: replace raw token string collection in WHERE clause predicate (line 440) with parsed expression using p.parseExpr(). Replace manual parenthesis depth tracking in ADD/DROP audit spec actions (lines 481-539) with structured AuditSpecAction AST nodes containing action type and group list instead of string concatenation.

batches.name: event_notification_options_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 135 gap: replace nodes.String formatting in parseEventNotificationOptions() (lines 75-155) with structured EventNotificationOption AST nodes (scope type, event group list, target service/broker instance) instead of manual ON=SERVER/FOR=xxx/SERVICE=xxx string formatting.

batches.name: resource_governor_outer_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 102 gap: replace nodes.String key=value string concatenation in parseResourceGovernorOptions() outer loop (lines 351-390) with structured ResourceGovernorOption AST nodes. The WITH clause inner parsing is structured but outer USING pool_name, RECONFIGURE, DISABLE, qualified name options still use string nodes.

batches.name: external_cleanup_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 124 gap: remove dead parseNestedParens() function (lines 971-1020, defined but never called). Replace nodes.String formatting in parseExternalSetOptions() (lines 665-690) with structured ExternalOption AST nodes (key, value) instead of key=value string concatenation.

batches.name: availability_skip_cleanup_depth
batches.description: Fix batch 125 gap: replace p.advance() // skip unexpected tokens at lines 433, 502, 567 with structured error handling or explicit unknown-token error recovery. These default-case token skips silently consume malformed input without raising parse errors.
